seoTitle: Create a Delivery Note (Packing Slip) in QuickBooks
metaDescription: How to generate a delivery note or packing slip from an existing invoice in QuickBooks Online, without exposing pricing to customers.
intro: Customers receiving a shipment need an itemized list of what's inside — not your pricing or billing terms. QuickBooks Online can convert an existing invoice or sales receipt into a clean delivery note that strips out dollar amounts automatically. Here's how to generate one, either individually or in a batch.

steps.heading: Find your target invoice
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Invoices or All Sales, and locate the transaction tied to the shipment.

steps.heading: Confirm the item details
steps.body: Open the invoice and check that shipped items and quantities are accurate before generating any paperwork.

steps.heading: Open the print options
steps.body: Click Print or Preview at the bottom of the invoice screen to see the document options available.

steps.heading: Select Print Packing Slip
steps.body: Choose the Print Packing Slip or Delivery Note option from the pop-up menu to generate the pricing-free version.

steps.heading: Print or batch process
steps.body: Print the single slip directly, or return to the Sales grid, select multiple orders, and use Batch Actions to print packing slips for all of them at once.

faq.question: Will the delivery note show customer pricing?
faq.answer: No, the packing slip format automatically hides pricing and billing totals, showing only items and quantities.

faq.question: Can I print delivery notes for multiple orders at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the batch action from the Sales grid to select several pending orders and print all their packing slips together.

faq.question: Do I need a separate invoice and delivery note?
faq.answer: Yes, the delivery note is generated from the invoice but is a distinct pricing-free document meant to travel with the shipment.

faq.question: How do I avoid margin issues when printing packing slips?
faq.answer: Set your printer to Actual Size in the page layout settings so item lists don't shift or get cut off.
